criteria.label: Solve for eigenvalues and one eigenvector of a 2×2 matrix by hand.
criteria.example: For A = [[1, 2], [0, 3]], eigenvalues are 1 and 3; for λ=1, solve (A-I)v=0 to get v proportional to (1, 0).

criteria.label: Compute eigenpairs with NumPy and verify Av = λv numerically.
criteria.example: Use `np.linalg.eig` on a 3×3 matrix and assert `np.allclose(A @ v, lam * v)` for each column eigenvector v.

criteria.label: Interpret eigenvalues of a covariance matrix as variance along principal directions.
criteria.example: Given eigenvalues [9, 1] from `eigh(C)`, state that PC1 explains 9/(9+1) = 90% of variance and sketch the major axis direction.

criteria.label: Explain spectral radius and its relevance to iterative methods or RNN stability.
criteria.example: For W with eigenvalues 0.9 and -1.1, argue that repeated multiplication h ← Wh amplifies components along the -1.1 eigenvector because |λ| > 1.
